status: PENDING — awaiting researcher approval before execution
summary: Three schema changes to integrate the observation-question master catalogue with the session B findings table. (1) Create wa_obs_question_catalogue — the master catalogue as a database table. (2) Create wa_finding_catalogue_links — many-to-many junction table between findings and catalogue questions. (3) Add term_id and status columns to wa_session_b_findings. Note: delete_flag already exists on wa_session_b_findings from the 2026-04-15 lifecycle update — no duplicate column needed.
motivation: The observation-question master catalogue (194 questions, v2.0) has been developed as the analytical instrument for Session B. The 171 session B findings have been classified against the catalogue: 77 findings have been mapped to catalogue questions, 22 are word-specific data flags, and 3 have no current catalogue coverage (gap candidates). To make this mapping operational — queryable by question, by word, by status, and by coverage — the catalogue must exist in the database and the findings must be linkable to it. The junction table enables programme-level analysis: which questions have been asked of which words, which are unresolved, which findings have no question mapped. The term_id column on findings enables direct linkage to specific terms where the finding is triggered by term-level data rather than word-level observation. The status column on findings tracks the lifecycle: suggested (mapped by catalogue analysis, not yet verified against live data), validated (Session B has confirmed the mapping), pending (finding exists, no catalogue question mapped yet).

operations.sql:
CREATE TABLE wa_obs_question_catalogue (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    question_code       TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    section             TEXT    NOT NULL,
    source_word         TEXT    NOT NULL,
    source_registry_no  INTEGER,
    question_text       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    pattern_type        TEXT,
    date_added          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S','now')),
    catalogue_version   TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '2.0',
    active              INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
);

column_notes.question_code: Unique question identifier — Q001 through Q147, F-001 through F-014, L-001 through L-014, M-001 through M-011, C-001 through C-008. Future extensions use initial letter of source word.
column_notes.source_word: The word analysis from which this question was derived — 'grace', 'forgiveness', 'love', 'mercy', 'compassion'
column_notes.source_registry_no: Registry number of the source word — nullable for Q001-Q147 (grace, registry 68)
column_notes.question_text: Full word-neutral question text as in the master catalogue
column_notes.pattern_type: Generic pattern code this question addresses — P01 through P18 where known, NULL otherwise
column_notes.active: 1 = active standard question; 0 = deprecated or superseded

operations.sql:
CREATE TABLE wa_finding_catalogue_links (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    finding_id          INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES wa_session_b_findings(id),
    question_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES wa_obs_question_catalogue(id),
    coverage            TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PARTIAL'
                        CHECK (coverage IN ('FULL', 'PARTIAL')),
    status              TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'suggested'
                        CHECK (status IN ('suggested', 'validated', 'rejected')),
    pattern_type        TEXT,
    mapped_date         TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S','now')),
    validated_date      TEXT,
    validated_by        TEXT,
    session_b_note      TEXT,
    delete_flag         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

column_notes.coverage: FULL = catalogue question addresses the finding squarely; PARTIAL = opens the right direction but finding requires further analytical work beyond the question
column_notes.status: suggested = mapped by catalogue analysis, not yet verified against live data; validated = Session B has reviewed and confirmed the mapping; rejected = Session B has determined the mapping is incorrect or not applicable
column_notes.pattern_type: Generic pattern code (P01-P18) — may differ from the question's pattern_type where a finding instantiates a pattern that multiple questions touch
column_notes.session_b_note: Free text note added by Session B analyst when validating or rejecting — records the specific outcome for this finding-question pair

indexes: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_fcl_finding_question ON wa_finding_catalogue_links(finding_id, question_id);
operations.note: The unique index on (finding_id, question_id) prevents duplicate mappings. A finding can link to multiple questions (one row per question). A question can link to multiple findings across the corpus.

operations.action: ALTER TABLE wa_session_b_findings — add term_id and status columns

columns_to_add.sql: ALTER TABLE wa_session_b_findings ADD COLUMN term_id INTEGER REFERENCES mti_terms(id);
columns_to_add.note: FK to mti_terms — nullable. Populated where the finding is triggered by a specific term's data rather than a word-level observation. Most Dimension Review findings are word-level (term_id NULL). Term-behaviour and etymology findings typically have a specific term.

columns_to_add.sql: ALTER TABLE wa_session_b_findings ADD COLUMN status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'in_review', 'complete'));
columns_to_add.note: Tracks the finding's lifecycle in Session B. pending = finding exists, not yet addressed in Session B; in_review = Session B is actively working this word; complete = finding has been resolved (either absorbed into catalogue question with answer, or closed as obsolete). Note: delete_flag already exists from the 2026-04-15 lifecycle update and covers the deleted/obsolete state — status tracks the review lifecycle separately.

population_plan.wa_obs_question_catalogue: Populate from wa-global-obs-question-master-catalogue-v2_0-20260415.md. 194 rows. One row per question code. Section mapping: Q001-020 = S1-Characteristic, Q021-041 = S2-Impact, Q042-085 = S3-Verse, Q086-121 = S4-Vocabulary, Q122-147 = S5-Connections, F-001-014 = EXT-Forgiveness, L-001-014 = EXT-Love, M-001-011 = EXT-Mercy, C-001-008 = EXT-Compassion. Population to be done by Claude AI in a separate session once the table exists.
population_plan.wa_finding_catalogue_links: Populate from wa-global-sessionb-catalogue-mapped-extract-v1-20260415.json. 77 records with finding_id, question codes, coverage ratings and pattern types. Status = 'suggested' for all initial rows. Population requires question_id lookup against wa_obs_question_catalogue by question_code — Claude AI to produce the insert statements once both tables exist.
population_plan.wa_session_b_findings_status: Set status = 'pending' for all existing rows (this is the DEFAULT so no explicit update needed). term_id remains NULL for all existing rows pending term-level review.

verification_queries: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_obs_question_catalogue WHERE active = 1; -- expect 194
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_finding_catalogue_links; -- expect 77 after initial population
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_finding_catalogue_links WHERE status = 'suggested'; -- expect 77
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_finding_catalogue_links WHERE coverage = 'FULL'; -- expect 38
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_finding_catalogue_links WHERE coverage = 'PARTIAL'; -- expect 36
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT question_id) FROM wa_finding_catalogue_links; -- number of distinct questions that have at least one finding mapped

SELECT q.question_code, COUNT(l.id) as finding_count FROM wa_obs_question_catalogue q LEFT JOIN wa_finding_catalogue_links l ON q.id = l.question_id GROUP BY q.id ORDER BY finding_count DESC LIMIT 10; -- most-referenced questions

SELECT f.finding_id, f.word FROM wa_session_b_findings f LEFT JOIN wa_finding_catalogue_links l ON f.id = l.finding_id WHERE l.id IS NULL AND f.delete_flag = 0; -- findings with no catalogue question mapped

key_analytical_queries_enabled.findings_without_question: SELECT f.* FROM wa_session_b_findings f LEFT JOIN wa_finding_catalogue_links l ON f.id = l.finding_id WHERE l.id IS NULL AND f.delete_flag = 0
key_analytical_queries_enabled.question_coverage_by_word: SELECT f.word, q.question_code, l.coverage, l.status FROM wa_finding_catalogue_links l JOIN wa_session_b_findings f ON f.id = l.finding_id JOIN wa_obs_question_catalogue q ON q.id = l.question_id ORDER BY f.word, q.question_code
key_analytical_queries_enabled.unresolved_findings_for_word: SELECT f.* FROM wa_session_b_findings f WHERE f.registry_id = :registry_id AND f.status != 'complete' AND f.delete_flag = 0
key_analytical_queries_enabled.questions_not_yet_triggered: SELECT q.question_code, q.question_text FROM wa_obs_question_catalogue q LEFT JOIN wa_finding_catalogue_links l ON q.id = l.question_id WHERE l.id IS NULL AND q.active = 1
key_analytical_queries_enabled.programme_coverage_heatmap: SELECT q.section, q.question_code, COUNT(l.id) as finding_count, SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='validated' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as validated FROM wa_obs_question_catalogue q LEFT JOIN wa_finding_catalogue_links l ON q.id = l.question_id GROUP BY q.id ORDER BY q.section, q.question_code

schema_version_note: Increment schema version to 3.9.0 in schema documentation after execution. Update wa-global-general-rules to note the new tables in the programme data architecture.
execution_order: Operations must be executed in order: (1) wa_obs_question_catalogue, (2) wa_finding_catalogue_links, (3) ALTER wa_session_b_findings. The junction table references both parent tables so both must exist before it is created.
rollback_note: SQLite does not support DROP COLUMN. If term_id or status columns need to be removed, a table rebuild is required. Confirm the column definitions are correct before executing operation 3.
